title: Magnificently Grilled Syrian Wagyu Kebabs
description: A traditional Syrian dish featuring Wagyu beef, this recipe is a flavorful and adventurous kebab dish, perfect for a dinner party. The combination of spices and grill marks creates a jammy and shaggy texture.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, sumac, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the Wagyu beef cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated beef cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 130 degrees Fahrenheit for medium-rare.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a side of grilled vegetables or pita bread.
Traditionally, Syrian kebabs are served with a side of ambah, a spicy mango pickle, for added flavor and texture.
